extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from a 4-rider breakaway that held off a disorganized peloton missing key sprint trains after horrific weather caused chaos and abandoned chases
tactical_patterns: Four-rider breakaway survived because sprint teams exhausted domestiques on clothing changes and crash repairs in apocalyptic conditions
Intermediate sprint at 150km caused peloton splits that shed GC riders when teams accelerated in treacherous weather
Clothing change logistics cost Gaviria contact with peloton, revealing wardrobe strategy risk on transitional stages in rain
Breakaway attack patterns inside 1500m failed as Gee went too early; Cort held powder dry until 300m

rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: lost contact with Maglia Rosa group during Caruso-Milan chase, hemorrhaged 10 minutes and top-10 GC position
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: abandoned while ill within 2 hours of racing, showing vulnerability to post-rest day illness
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: crashed immediately after clothing change, suggesting team failed to manage his re-entry to peloton safely
rider_signals.GEE Derek: neutral: attacked at 1500m and 300m in breakaway sprint but both times lacked finishing power against Cort
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: regained peloton contact despite disorganized teams, suggesting strong climbing form for reduced bunch sprint
